assistant #228 commentary 02:18:13

底层逻辑已经接上了，我现在补两类回归测试：

- memorytree 分支不存在时，确认会主动 worktree add -b memorytree
- GitHub SSH push 失败时，确认会自动回退到 HTTPS，并且失败信息会带上主用/回退 URL

这样这次 PR 的“防呆点”就都有测试护住。

Tool Call #229 apply_patch call_R7SBjW6qvUZv2PH8xQoW9Umz 02:18:50
apply_patch input=*** Begin Patch *** Update File: C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\worktree.test.ts @@ describe('ensureProjectWorktree', () => { it('accepts equivale...
Show input payload 
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\worktree.test.ts
@@
 describe('ensureProjectWorktree', () => {
   it('accepts equivalent git common-dir paths that differ by alias or canonical form', async () => {
@@
     expect(result).toEqual({
       branch: 'memorytree',
       created: false,
     })
   })
+
+  it('creates the memorytree branch automatically when the dedicated worktree does not exist yet', async () => {
+    const freshMemoryPath = join(sandboxRoot, 'fresh-memory-worktree')
+    const gitMock = vi.fn((cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+      if (cwd === developmentPath && args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--show-toplevel') {
+        return `${developmentPath}\n`
+      }
+      if (
+        cwd === developmentPath
+        && args[0] === 'worktree'
+        && args[1] === 'add'
+        && args[2] === '-b'
+        && args[3] === 'memorytree'
+        && args[4] === freshMemoryPath
+        && args[5] === 'HEAD'
+      ) {
+        return ''
+      }
+      throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${cwd} ${args.join(' ')}`)
+    })
+
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: gitMock,
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--git-common-dir' && options?.cwd === developmentPath) {
+          return `${commonDir}\n`
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'check-ref-format' && args[1] === '--branch' && args[2] === 'memorytree') {
+          return 'memorytree\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--verify') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureProjectWorktree } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    const result = ensureProjectWorktree({
+      development_path: developmentPath,
+      memory_path: freshMemoryPath,
+      memory_branch: 'memorytree',
+    } as never)
+
+    expect(result).toEqual({
+      branch: 'memorytree',
+      created: true,
+    })
+    expect(gitMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      developmentPath,
+      'worktree',
+      'add',
+      '-b',
+      'memorytree',
+      freshMemoryPath,
+      'HEAD',
+    )
+  })
+})
+
+describe('ensureBranchUpstream', () => {
+  it('falls back from GitHub SSH push URLs to HTTPS when the SSH push fails', async () => {
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+        if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote') {
+          return 'origin\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
+          return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
+          return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        if (
+          args[0] === '-c'
+          && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
+          && args[2] === 'push'
+          && args[3] === '-u'
+          && args[4] === 'origin'
+          && args[5] === 'memorytree'
+        ) {
+          return ''
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    const result = ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
+
+    expect(result).toEqual({
+      remote: 'origin',
+      created: true,
+      pushUrl: 'https://github.com/example/repo.git',
+      transport: 'https',
+      usedFallback: true,
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('reports both the primary and fallback push failures when fallback also fails', async () => {
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+        if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote') {
+          return 'origin\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
+          return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
+          return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        if (
+          args[0] === '-c'
+          && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
+          && args[2] === 'push'
+          && args[3] === '-u'
+          && args[4] === 'origin'
+          && args[5] === 'memorytree'
+        ) {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git -c remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Authentication failed for https://github.com/example/repo.git\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    expect(() => ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')).toThrowError(
+      /Primary push URL: ssh:\/\/git@ssh\.github\.com:443\/example\/repo\.git/,
+    )
+    expect(() => ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')).toThrowError(
+      /Fallback push URL: https:\/\/github\.com\/example\/repo\.git/,
+    )
+  })
 })
*** End Patch

   it('upserts config and ensures a dedicated worktree', async () => {
@@
     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Worktree branch: memorytree')
+    expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Push remote: origin')
+    expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Push URL: https://github.com/example/repo.git')
     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Upstream configured: yes (origin/memorytree)')
   })
+
+  it('reports when upstream setup succeeds via a fallback push URL', async () => {
+    const savedConfigs: unknown[] = []
+    const upserted = {
+      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
+      auto_push: true,
+      log_level: 'info',
+      watch_dirs: [],
+      generate_report: false,
+      ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
+      locale: 'en',
+      gh_pages_branch: '',
+      cname: '',
+      webhook_url: '',
+      report_base_url: '',

+        {
+          id: 'repo',
+          path: '/memorytree/worktrees/repo',
+          name: 'repo',
+          development_path: '/repo',
+          memory_path: '/memorytree/worktrees/repo',
+          memory_branch: 'memorytree',

+          auto_push: true,
+          generate_report: true,
+          ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
+          locale: 'en',
+          gh_pages_branch: '',
+          cname: '',
+          webhook_url: '',
+          report_base_url: '',
+          report_port: 10010,
+          last_heartbeat_at: '',
+        },
+      ],
+    }
+
+    vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/config.js', () => ({
+      DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH: 'memorytree',
+      DEFAULT_RAW_UPLOAD_PERMISSION: 'not-set',
+      normalizeRawUploadPermission: mockNormalizeRawUploadPermission,
+      configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
+      loadConfig: () => ({
+        heartbeat_interval: '9m',
+        auto_push: false,
+        projects: [],
+        watch_dirs: [],
+        log_level: 'info',
+        generate_report: false,
+        ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
+        locale: 'en',
+        gh_pages_branch: '',
+        cname: '',
+        webhook_url: '',
+        report_base_url: '',
+        report_port: 10010,

+      saveConfig: (cfg: unknown) => { savedConfigs.push(cfg) },
+      intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
+      findProjectForPath: () => upserted.projects[0],
+      upsertProject: () => upserted,
+    }))
+    vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/lock.js', () => ({
+      readLockPid: () => null,

+    vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js', () => ({

+        remote: 'origin',
+        fetchUrl: 'https://github.com/example/repo.git',
+        pushUrl: 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git',
+        transport: 'ssh',
+        fallbackUrls: ['https://github.com/example/repo.git'],

+      defaultProjectWorktreePath: () => '/memorytree/worktrees/repo',
+      defaultProjectWorktreeBranch: () => 'memorytree',

+        created: true,

+        transport: 'https',

+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({

+    }))
+

+    const result = mod.cmdRegisterProject({

+      quickStart: true,

Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
import { existsSync, mkdirSync, realpathSync } from 'node:fs'
import { basename, dirname, isAbsolute, resolve } from 'node:path'
import { platform } from 'node:process'

import type { ProjectEntry } from './config.js'
import { DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH, memorytreeRoot } from './config.js'
import { execCommand, git } from '../utils/exec.js'

export interface WorktreeStatus {
  readonly branch: string
  readonly created: boolean
}

export interface BranchUpstreamStatus {
  readonly remote: string | null
  readonly created: boolean
  readonly pushUrl: string | null
  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
  readonly usedFallback: boolean
}

export type PushTransport = 'ssh' | 'https' | 'other'

export interface PushRemoteStatus {
  readonly remote: string | null
  readonly fetchUrl: string | null
  readonly pushUrl: string | null
  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
  readonly fallbackUrls: readonly string[]
}

export interface BranchPushStatus {
  readonly remote: string | null
  readonly pushUrl: string | null
  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
  readonly usedFallback: boolean
}

export function defaultProjectWorktreePath(developmentPath: string): string {
  return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'worktrees', slugifySegment(basename(resolve(developmentPath))))
}

export function defaultProjectWorktreeBranch(): string {
  return DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
}

export function resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>): string {
  const requested = project.memory_branch.trim()
  return isValidWorktreeBranchName(requested)
    ? requested
    : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
}

export function ensureProjectWorktree(project: ProjectEntry): WorktreeStatus {
  const developmentPath = resolve(project.development_path)
  const memoryPath = resolve(project.memory_path)
  if (samePath(developmentPath, memoryPath)) {
    return {
      branch: git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim(),
      created: false,
    }
  }

  const repoRoot = git(developmentPath, 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel').trim()
  const repoCommonDir = gitCommonDir(developmentPath)
  const branch = resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project)

  if (existsSync(memoryPath)) {
    const existingRoot = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'], {
      cwd: memoryPath,
      allowFailure: true,
    }).trim()
    if (!existingRoot) {
      throw new Error(`Configured memory_path is not a git worktree: ${memoryPath}`)
    }
    const existingCommonDir = gitCommonDir(memoryPath)
    if (!samePath(existingCommonDir, repoCommonDir)) {
      throw new Error(`Configured memory_path belongs to a different repository: ${memoryPath}`)
    }

    const current = git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
    if (current !== branch) {
      if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
        git(memoryPath, 'checkout', branch)
      } else {
        git(memoryPath, 'checkout', '-b', branch)
      }
    }

    return { branch, created: false }
  }

  mkdirSync(dirname(memoryPath), { recursive: true })
  if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
    git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', memoryPath, branch)
  } else {
    git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', '-b', branch, memoryPath, 'HEAD')
  }

  return { branch, created: true }
}

export function ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchUpstreamStatus {
  const target = describePushRemote(projectPath)
  if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
    return {
      remote: null,
      created: false,
      pushUrl: null,
      transport: null,
      usedFallback: false,
    }
  }
  if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
    return {
      remote: target.remote,
      created: false,
      pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
      transport: target.transport,
      usedFallback: false,
    }
  }

  const pushed = pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, target, true)
  return {
    remote: pushed.remote,
    created: true,
    pushUrl: pushed.pushUrl,
    transport: pushed.transport,
    usedFallback: pushed.usedFallback,
  }
}

export function pushBranchToRemote(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchPushStatus {
  const remote = resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath) ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
  const target = describePushRemote(projectPath, remote ?? undefined)
  if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
    return {
      remote: null,
      pushUrl: null,
      transport: null,
      usedFallback: false,
    }
  }

  return pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, target, false)
}

export function describePushRemote(projectPath: string, remoteName?: string): PushRemoteStatus {
  const remote = remoteName ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
  if (remote === null) {
    return {
      remote: null,
      fetchUrl: null,
      pushUrl: null,
      transport: null,
      fallbackUrls: [],
    }
  }

  const fetchUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', remote], {
    cwd: projectPath,
    allowFailure: true,
  }).trim()
  const pushUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', '--push', remote], {
    cwd: projectPath,
    allowFailure: true,
  }).trim() || fetchUrl
  const transport = pushUrl ? detectPushTransport(pushUrl) : null

  return {
    remote,
    fetchUrl: fetchUrl || null,
    pushUrl: pushUrl || null,
    transport,
    fallbackUrls: collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl, fetchUrl),
  }
}

export function resolvePushRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
  const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
    .split(/\r?\n/)
    .map(remote => remote.trim())
    .filter(Boolean)

  if (remotes.length === 0) {
    return null
  }
  if (remotes.includes('origin')) {
    return 'origin'
  }
  return remotes[0] ?? null
}

export function hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath: string): boolean {
  const output = execCommand(
    'git',
    ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
    { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
  )
  return output.trim().length > 0
}

export function isValidWorktreeBranchName(branch: string): boolean {
  if (!branch || branch.trim() !== branch) {
    return false
  }

  const output = execCommand('git', ['check-ref-format', '--branch', branch], { allowFailure: true })
  return output.trim() === branch
}

export function isProjectMemoryBranch(
  branch: string,
  project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
): boolean {
  const normalized = branch.trim()
  if (!normalized) return false

  const expected = project ? resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project) : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  return normalized === expected
    || normalized === DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
    || normalized.startsWith(`${DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH}/`)
}

function gitCommonDir(cwd: string): string {
  const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--git-common-dir'], {
    cwd,
  }).trim()

  if (!output) {
    throw new Error(`Unable to resolve git common-dir for: ${cwd}`)
  }

  return isAbsolute(output) ? resolve(output) : resolve(cwd, output)
}

function resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
  const upstream = execCommand(
    'git',
    ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
    { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
  ).trim()
  if (!upstream) {
    return null
  }

  const slashIndex = upstream.indexOf('/')
  return slashIndex <= 0 ? null : upstream.slice(0, slashIndex)
}

function pushToResolvedRemote(
  projectPath: string,
  branch: string,
  target: PushRemoteStatus & { remote: string; pushUrl: string; transport: PushTransport },
  setUpstream: boolean,
): BranchPushStatus {
  const pushArgs = setUpstream
    ? ['push', '-u', target.remote, branch]
    : ['push', target.remote, branch]

  try {
    git(projectPath, ...pushArgs)
    return {
      remote: target.remote,
      pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
      transport: target.transport,
      usedFallback: false,
    }
  } catch (primaryError: unknown) {
    if (shouldTryFallbackTransport(primaryError, target.fallbackUrls)) {
      for (const fallbackUrl of target.fallbackUrls) {
        try {
          execCommand(
            'git',
            ['-c', `remote.${target.remote}.pushurl=${fallbackUrl}`, ...pushArgs],
            { cwd: projectPath },
          )
          return {
            remote: target.remote,
            pushUrl: fallbackUrl,
            transport: detectPushTransport(fallbackUrl),
            usedFallback: true,
          }
        } catch (fallbackError: unknown) {
          throw new Error(formatPushFailure(target.remote, target.pushUrl, primaryError, fallbackUrl, fallbackError))
        }
      }
    }

    throw new Error(formatPushFailure(target.remote, target.pushUrl, primaryError))
  }
}

function detectPushTransport(url: string): PushTransport {
  const normalized = url.trim().toLowerCase()
  if (normalized.startsWith('https://')) {
    return 'https'
  }
  if (normalized.startsWith('ssh://') || normalized.startsWith('git@')) {
    return 'ssh'
  }
  return 'other'
}

function collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl: string, fetchUrl: string): string[] {
  const candidates = [
    fetchUrl,
    ...alternateTransportUrls(pushUrl),
    ...alternateTransportUrls(fetchUrl),
  ]

  const seen = new Set<string>()
  const normalizedPrimary = normalizeComparableUrl(pushUrl)
  const fallbackUrls: string[] = []

  for (const candidate of candidates) {
    const trimmed = candidate.trim()
    if (!trimmed) continue

    const normalizedCandidate = normalizeComparableUrl(trimmed)
    if (normalizedCandidate === normalizedPrimary || seen.has(normalizedCandidate)) {
      continue
    }

    seen.add(normalizedCandidate)
    fallbackUrls.push(trimmed)
  }

  return fallbackUrls
}

function alternateTransportUrls(url: string): string[] {
  const repo = parseGitHubRepo(url)
  if (repo === null) {
    return []
  }

  const httpsUrl = `https://github.com/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
  const sshUrl = `ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`

  return [httpsUrl, sshUrl]
}

function parseGitHubRepo(url: string): { owner: string; repo: string } | null {
  const trimmed = url.trim()
  if (!trimmed) {
    return null
  }

  const httpsMatch = trimmed.match(/^https:\/\/github\.com\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
  if (httpsMatch) {
    return {
      owner: httpsMatch[1]!,
      repo: httpsMatch[2]!,
    }
  }

  const scpMatch = trimmed.match(/^git@github\.com:([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?$/i)
  if (scpMatch) {
    return {
      owner: scpMatch[1]!,
      repo: scpMatch[2]!,
    }
  }

  const sshMatch = trimmed.match(/^ssh:\/\/git@(?:github\.com|ssh\.github\.com(?::443)?)\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
  if (sshMatch) {
    return {
      owner: sshMatch[1]!,
      repo: sshMatch[2]!,
    }
  }

  return null
}

function shouldTryFallbackTransport(error: unknown, fallbackUrls: readonly string[]): boolean {
  if (fallbackUrls.length === 0) {
    return false
  }

  const message = collectErrorText(error).toLowerCase()
  return [
    'host key verification failed',
    'permission denied (publickey)',
    'could not read from remote repository',
    'authentication failed',
    'repository not found',
    'could not resolve host',
    'unable to access',
    'connection timed out',
    'connection refused',
    'ssh:',
  ].some(fragment => message.includes(fragment))
}

function formatPushFailure(
  remote: string,
  pushUrl: string,
  primaryError: unknown,
  fallbackUrl?: string,
  fallbackError?: unknown,
): string {
  const lines = [
    `Unable to push branch to remote '${remote}'.`,
    `Primary push URL: ${pushUrl}`,
    `Primary error: ${summarizeError(primaryError)}`,
  ]

  if (fallbackUrl) {
    lines.push(`Fallback push URL: ${fallbackUrl}`)
    if (fallbackError) {
      lines.push(`Fallback error: ${summarizeError(fallbackError)}`)
    }
  }

  return lines.join('\n')
}

function summarizeError(error: unknown): string {
  const lines = collectErrorText(error)
    .split(/\r?\n/)
    .map(line => line.trim())
    .filter(Boolean)

  const preferred = lines.find(line => !line.startsWith('Command failed:'))
  return preferred ?? lines[0] ?? String(error)
}

function collectErrorText(error: unknown): string {
  if (error instanceof Error) {
    const parts = [error.message]
    const extra = error as {
      readonly stdout?: string | Buffer
      readonly stderr?: string | Buffer
    }
    if (extra.stdout !== undefined) {
      parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stdout))
    }
    if (extra.stderr !== undefined) {
      parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stderr))
    }
    return parts.filter(Boolean).join('\n')
  }

  return String(error)
}

function bufferToString(value: string | Buffer): string {
  return typeof value === 'string' ? value : value.toString('utf-8')
}

function normalizeComparableUrl(value: string): string {
  return value.trim().replace(/\/+$/, '').toLowerCase()
}

function branchExists(repoRoot: string, branch: string): boolean {
  const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--verify', `refs/heads/${branch}`], {
    cwd: repoRoot,
    allowFailure: true,
  })
  return output.trim().length > 0
}

function samePath(left: string, right: string): boolean {
  return normalizePath(left) === normalizePath(right)
}

function normalizePath(value: string): string {
  let normalizedPath = resolve(value)
  if (existsSync(normalizedPath)) {
    try {
      normalizedPath = realpathSync.native
        ? realpathSync.native(normalizedPath)
        : realpathSync(normalizedPath)
    } catch {
      // Fall back to the resolved path when realpath fails.
    }
  }

  const normalized = normalizedPath.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '')
  return platform === 'win32' ? normalized.toLowerCase() : normalized
}

function slugifySegment(value: string): string {
  const slug = value
    .trim()
    .toLowerCase()
    .replace(/[^a-z0-9._-]+/g, '-')
    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    .replace(/-{2,}/g, '-')

  return slug || 'project'
}

assistant #291 final_answer 02:21:37

改动已经在 `memorytree-workflow` 本地起好了，核心落在 [worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L103)、[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#...

Expand full message

改动已经在 memorytree-workflow 本地起好了，核心落在 [worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L103)、[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L213) 和 [heartbeat.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts#L347)。

这版 PR 做了三件事：
- 明确兜住“用户一般不会先建 memorytree 分支”这个场景，并补了回归测试，[worktree.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts#L78)。
- quick-start/register 现在会输出实际 Push remote 和 Push URL，并在 fallback 生效时明确提示，[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L234)、[daemon.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/cli/daemon.test.ts#L239)。
- upstream/push 遇到 GitHub SSH 问题时，会自动尝试 HTTPS，heartbeat 日常 push 也复用同一套逻辑，[worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L140)、[heartbeat.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts#L352)、[branch-safety.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts#L123)。

已验证：
- npm run typecheck
- npm test -- --run tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts tests/cli/daemon.test.ts

注意一件事：仓库里原本就有一个未归属的 package-lock.json 改动，我没有动它。真正起 PR 时建议只包含这 6 个文件，不把 package-lock.json 混进去。

PR 草稿可以直接用这个：

Title:
memorytree(daemon): add safer upstream setup and GitHub transport fallback

Summary:
- show push remote and push URL during daemon quick-start/register
- keep dedicated memorytree branch creation automatic and cover it with tests
- fall back between GitHub SSH and HTTPS when the first upstream push transport fails
- reuse the same fallback logic for heartbeat auto-push

Why:
Quick Start currently fails hard on first upstream setup when the repo push URL uses SSH and the machine has not trusted that host yet. Users also cannot easily tell which remote URL MemoryTree is actually trying to push to. This makes the happy path feel brittle even when local registration and worktree setup already succeeded.

Testing:
- npm run typecheck
- npm test -- --run tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts tests/cli/daemon.test.ts

Notes:
- keep package-lock.json out of this PR; it was already dirty before these changes

如果你要，我下一步可以继续帮你整理成一版更偏 issue/PR 风格的英文说明，或者直接给你一组 git add / git commit 建议命令。

task_completed #293 019d22c3-24c9-77f3-871e-b4ebb75ef142 02:21:37
改动已经在 `memorytree-workflow` 本地起好了，核心落在 [worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L103)、[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L213) 和 [heartbeat.t...
Show task summary

改动已经在 memorytree-workflow 本地起好了，核心落在 [worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L103)、[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L213) 和 [heartbeat.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts#L347)。

这版 PR 做了三件事：
- 明确兜住“用户一般不会先建 memorytree 分支”这个场景，并补了回归测试，[worktree.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts#L78)。
- quick-start/register 现在会输出实际 Push remote 和 Push URL，并在 fallback 生效时明确提示，[cmd-daemon.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L234)、[daemon.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/cli/daemon.test.ts#L239)。
- upstream/push 遇到 GitHub SSH 问题时，会自动尝试 HTTPS，heartbeat 日常 push 也复用同一套逻辑，[worktree.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts#L140)、[heartbeat.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts#L352)、[branch-safety.test.ts](/C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts#L123)。

已验证：
- npm run typecheck
- npm test -- --run tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts tests/cli/daemon.test.ts

注意一件事：仓库里原本就有一个未归属的 package-lock.json 改动，我没有动它。真正起 PR 时建议只包含这 6 个文件，不把 package-lock.json 混进去。

PR 草稿可以直接用这个：

Title:
memorytree(daemon): add safer upstream setup and GitHub transport fallback

Summary:
- show push remote and push URL during daemon quick-start/register
- keep dedicated memorytree branch creation automatic and cover it with tests
- fall back between GitHub SSH and HTTPS when the first upstream push transport fails
- reuse the same fallback logic for heartbeat auto-push

Why:
Quick Start currently fails hard on first upstream setup when the repo push URL uses SSH and the machine has not trusted that host yet. Users also cannot easily tell which remote URL MemoryTree is actually trying to push to. This makes the happy path feel brittle even when local registration and worktree setup already succeeded.

Testing:
- npm run typecheck
- npm test -- --run tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts tests/heartbeat/branch-safety.test.ts tests/cli/daemon.test.ts

Notes:
- keep package-lock.json out of this PR; it was already dirty before these changes

如果你要，我下一步可以继续帮你整理成一版更偏 issue/PR 风格的英文说明，或者直接给你一组 git add / git commit 建议命令。

 export interface BranchUpstreamStatus {
   readonly remote: string | null
   readonly created: boolean
+  readonly pushUrl: string | null
+  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
+  readonly usedFallback: boolean
+}
+
+export type PushTransport = 'ssh' | 'https' | 'other'
+
+export interface PushRemoteStatus {
+  readonly remote: string | null
+  readonly fetchUrl: string | null
+  readonly pushUrl: string | null
+  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
+  readonly fallbackUrls: readonly string[]
+}
+
+export interface BranchPushStatus {
+  readonly remote: string | null
+  readonly pushUrl: string | null
+  readonly transport: PushTransport | null
+  readonly usedFallback: boolean
 }
 
 export function defaultProjectWorktreePath(developmentPath: string): string {
@@ -81,16 +101,92 @@ export function ensureProjectWorktree(project: ProjectEntry): WorktreeStatus {
 }
 
 export function ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchUpstreamStatus {
-  const remote = resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
-  if (remote === null) {
-    return { remote: null, created: false }
+  const target = describePushRemote(projectPath)
+  if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
+    return {
+      remote: null,
+      created: false,
+      pushUrl: null,
+      transport: null,
+      usedFallback: false,
+    }
   }
   if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
-    return { remote, created: false }
+    return {
+      remote: target.remote,
+      created: false,
+      pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
+      transport: target.transport,
+      usedFallback: false,
+    }
+  }
+
+  const resolvedTarget = {
+    ...target,
+    remote: target.remote,
+    pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
+    transport: target.transport,
+  }
+  const pushed = pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, resolvedTarget, true)
+  return {
+    remote: pushed.remote,
+    created: true,
+    pushUrl: pushed.pushUrl,
+    transport: pushed.transport,
+    usedFallback: pushed.usedFallback,
+  }
+}
+
+export function pushBranchToRemote(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchPushStatus {
+  const remote = resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath) ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
+  const target = describePushRemote(projectPath, remote ?? undefined)
+  if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
+    return {
+      remote: null,
+      pushUrl: null,
+      transport: null,
+      usedFallback: false,
+    }
+  }
+
+  const resolvedTarget = {
+    ...target,
+    remote: target.remote,
+    pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
+    transport: target.transport,
+  }
+  return pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, resolvedTarget, false)
+}
+
+export function describePushRemote(projectPath: string, remoteName?: string): PushRemoteStatus {
+  const remote = remoteName ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
+  if (remote === null) {
+    return {
+      remote: null,
+      fetchUrl: null,
+      pushUrl: null,
+      transport: null,
+      fallbackUrls: [],
+    }
   }
 
-  git(projectPath, 'push', '-u', remote, branch)
-  return { remote, created: true }
+  const fetchUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', remote], {
+    cwd: projectPath,
+    allowFailure: true,
+  }).trim()
+  const pushUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', '--push', remote], {
+    cwd: projectPath,
+    allowFailure: true,
+  }).trim() || fetchUrl
+  const transport = pushUrl ? detectPushTransport(pushUrl) : null
+
+  return {
+    remote,
+    fetchUrl: fetchUrl || null,
+    pushUrl: pushUrl || null,
+    transport,
+    fallbackUrls: collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl, fetchUrl),
+  }
 }
 
 export function resolvePushRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
@@ -151,6 +247,241 @@ function gitCommonDir(cwd: string): string {
   return isAbsolute(output) ? resolve(output) : resolve(cwd, output)
 }
 
+function resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
+  const upstream = execCommand(
+    'git',
+    ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
+    { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
+  ).trim()
+  if (!upstream) {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  const slashIndex = upstream.indexOf('/')
+  return slashIndex <= 0 ? null : upstream.slice(0, slashIndex)
+}
+
+function pushToResolvedRemote(
+  projectPath: string,
+  branch: string,
+  target: PushRemoteStatus & { remote: string; pushUrl: string; transport: PushTransport },
+  setUpstream: boolean,
+): BranchPushStatus {
+  const pushArgs = setUpstream
+    ? ['push', '-u', target.remote, branch]
+    : ['push', target.remote, branch]
+
+  try {
+    git(projectPath, ...pushArgs)
+    return {
+      remote: target.remote,
+      pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
+      transport: target.transport,
+      usedFallback: false,
+    }
+  } catch (primaryError: unknown) {
+    if (shouldTryFallbackTransport(primaryError, target.fallbackUrls)) {
+      let lastFallbackFailure: { url: string; error: unknown } | null = null
+      for (const fallbackUrl of target.fallbackUrls) {
+        try {
+          execCommand(
+            'git',
+            ['-c', `remote.${target.remote}.pushurl=${fallbackUrl}`, ...pushArgs],
+            { cwd: projectPath },
+          )
+          return {
+            remote: target.remote,
+            pushUrl: fallbackUrl,
+            transport: detectPushTransport(fallbackUrl),
+            usedFallback: true,
+          }
+        } catch (fallbackError: unknown) {
+          lastFallbackFailure = {
+            url: fallbackUrl,
+            error: fallbackError,
+          }
+        }
+      }
+      if (lastFallbackFailure !== null) {
+        throw new Error(
+          formatPushFailure(
+            target.remote,
+            target.pushUrl,
+            primaryError,
+            lastFallbackFailure.url,
+            lastFallbackFailure.error,
+          ),
+        )
+      }
+    }
+
+    throw new Error(formatPushFailure(target.remote, target.pushUrl, primaryError))
+  }
+}
+
+function detectPushTransport(url: string): PushTransport {
+  const normalized = url.trim().toLowerCase()
+  if (normalized.startsWith('https://')) {
+    return 'https'
+  }
+  if (normalized.startsWith('ssh://') || normalized.startsWith('git@')) {
+    return 'ssh'
+  }
+  return 'other'
+}
+
+function collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl: string, fetchUrl: string): string[] {
+  const candidates = [
+    fetchUrl,
+    ...alternateTransportUrls(pushUrl),
+    ...alternateTransportUrls(fetchUrl),
+  ]
+
+  const seen = new Set<string>()
+  const normalizedPrimary = normalizeComparableUrl(pushUrl)
+  const fallbackUrls: string[] = []
+
+  for (const candidate of candidates) {
+    const trimmed = candidate.trim()
+    if (!trimmed) continue
+
+    const normalizedCandidate = normalizeComparableUrl(trimmed)
+    if (normalizedCandidate === normalizedPrimary || seen.has(normalizedCandidate)) {
+      continue
+    }
+
+    seen.add(normalizedCandidate)
+    fallbackUrls.push(trimmed)
+  }
+
+  return fallbackUrls
+}
+
+function alternateTransportUrls(url: string): string[] {
+  const repo = parseGitHubRepo(url)
+  if (repo === null) {
+    return []
+  }
+
+  const httpsUrl = `https://github.com/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
+  const sshUrl = `ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
+
+  return [httpsUrl, sshUrl]
+}
+
+function parseGitHubRepo(url: string): { owner: string; repo: string } | null {
+  const trimmed = url.trim()
+  if (!trimmed) {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  const httpsMatch = trimmed.match(/^https:\/\/github\.com\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
+  if (httpsMatch) {
+    return {
+      owner: httpsMatch[1]!,
+      repo: httpsMatch[2]!,
+    }
+  }
+
+  const scpMatch = trimmed.match(/^git@github\.com:([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?$/i)
+  if (scpMatch) {
+    return {
+      owner: scpMatch[1]!,
+      repo: scpMatch[2]!,
+    }
+  }
+
+  const sshMatch = trimmed.match(/^ssh:\/\/git@(?:github\.com|ssh\.github\.com(?::443)?)\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
+  if (sshMatch) {
+    return {
+      owner: sshMatch[1]!,
+      repo: sshMatch[2]!,
+    }
+  }
+
+  return null
+}
+
+function shouldTryFallbackTransport(error: unknown, fallbackUrls: readonly string[]): boolean {
+  if (fallbackUrls.length === 0) {
+    return false
+  }
+
+  const message = collectErrorText(error).toLowerCase()
+  return [
+    'host key verification failed',
+    'permission denied (publickey)',
+    'could not read from remote repository',
+    'authentication failed',
+    'repository not found',
+    'could not resolve host',
+    'unable to access',
+    'connection timed out',
+    'connection refused',
+    'ssh:',
+  ].some(fragment => message.includes(fragment))
+}
+
+function formatPushFailure(
+  remote: string,
+  pushUrl: string,
+  primaryError: unknown,
+  fallbackUrl?: string,
+  fallbackError?: unknown,
+): string {
+  const lines = [
+    `Unable to push branch to remote '${remote}'.`,
+    `Primary push URL: ${pushUrl}`,
+    `Primary error: ${summarizeError(primaryError)}`,
+  ]
+
+  if (fallbackUrl) {
+    lines.push(`Fallback push URL: ${fallbackUrl}`)
+    if (fallbackError) {
+      lines.push(`Fallback error: ${summarizeError(fallbackError)}`)
+    }
+  }
+
+  return lines.join('\n')
+}
+
+function summarizeError(error: unknown): string {
+  const lines = collectErrorText(error)
+    .split(/\r?\n/)
+    .map(line => line.trim())
+    .filter(Boolean)
+
+  const preferred = lines.find(line => !line.startsWith('Command failed:'))
+  return preferred ?? lines[0] ?? String(error)
+}
+
+function collectErrorText(error: unknown): string {
+  if (error instanceof Error) {
+    const parts = [error.message]
+    const extra = error as {
+      readonly stdout?: string | Buffer
+      readonly stderr?: string | Buffer
+    }
+    if (extra.stdout !== undefined) {
+      parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stdout))
+    }
+    if (extra.stderr !== undefined) {
+      parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stderr))
+    }
+    return parts.filter(Boolean).join('\n')
+  }
+
+  return String(error)
+}
+
+function bufferToString(value: string | Buffer): string {
+  return typeof value === 'string' ? value : value.toString('utf-8')
+}
+
+function normalizeComparableUrl(value: string): string {
+  return value.trim().replace(/\/+$/, '').toLowerCase()
+}
+
 function branchExists(repoRoot: string, branch: string): boolean {
   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--verify', `refs/heads/${branch}`], {
     cwd: repoRoot,

diff --git a/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts b/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts
index 6a0e1a1..3fff431 100644
--- a/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts
+++ b/tests/heartbeat/worktree.test.ts
@@ -74,4 +74,188 @@ describe('ensureProjectWorktree', () => {
       created: false,
     })
   })
+
+  it('creates the memorytree branch automatically when the dedicated worktree does not exist yet', async () => {
+    const freshMemoryPath = join(sandboxRoot, 'fresh-memory-worktree')
+    const gitMock = vi.fn((cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+      if (cwd === developmentPath && args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--show-toplevel') {
+        return `${developmentPath}\n`
+      }
+      if (
+        cwd === developmentPath
+        && args[0] === 'worktree'
+        && args[1] === 'add'
+        && args[2] === '-b'
+        && args[3] === 'memorytree'
+        && args[4] === freshMemoryPath
+        && args[5] === 'HEAD'
+      ) {
+        return ''
+      }
+      throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${cwd} ${args.join(' ')}`)
+    })
+
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: gitMock,
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--git-common-dir' && options?.cwd === developmentPath) {
+          return `${commonDir}\n`
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'check-ref-format' && args[1] === '--branch' && args[2] === 'memorytree') {
+          return 'memorytree\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--verify') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureProjectWorktree } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    const result = ensureProjectWorktree({
+      development_path: developmentPath,
+      memory_path: freshMemoryPath,
+      memory_branch: 'memorytree',
+    } as never)
+
+    expect(result).toEqual({
+      branch: 'memorytree',
+      created: true,
+    })
+    expect(gitMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      developmentPath,
+      'worktree',
+      'add',
+      '-b',
+      'memorytree',
+      freshMemoryPath,
+      'HEAD',
+    )
+  })
+})
+
+describe('ensureBranchUpstream', () => {
+  it('falls back from GitHub SSH push URLs to HTTPS when the SSH push fails', async () => {
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+        if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote') {
+          return 'origin\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
+          return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
+          return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        if (
+          args[0] === '-c'
+          && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
+          && args[2] === 'push'
+          && args[3] === '-u'
+          && args[4] === 'origin'
+          && args[5] === 'memorytree'
+        ) {
+          return ''
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    const result = ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
+
+    expect(result).toEqual({
+      remote: 'origin',
+      created: true,
+      pushUrl: 'https://github.com/example/repo.git',
+      transport: 'https',
+      usedFallback: true,
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('reports both the primary and fallback push failures when fallback also fails', async () => {
+    vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
+      git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
+        if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote') {
+          return 'origin\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+      execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
+        if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
+          throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
+          return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
+          return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
+        }
+        if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
+          return ''
+        }
+        if (
+          args[0] === '-c'
+          && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
+          && args[2] === 'push'
+          && args[3] === '-u'
+          && args[4] === 'origin'
+          && args[5] === 'memorytree'
+        ) {
+          const error = new Error('Command failed: git -c remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git push -u origin memorytree')
+          Object.assign(error, {
+            stderr: 'Authentication failed for https://github.com/example/repo.git\n',
+          })
+          throw error
+        }
+        throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
+      },
+    }))
+
+    const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
+
+    let thrown: unknown = null
+    try {
+      ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
+    } catch (error: unknown) {
+      thrown = error
+    }
+
+    expect(thrown).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
+    expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Primary push URL: ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git')
+    expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Fallback push URL: https://github.com/example/repo.git')
+    expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Host key verification failed.')
+    expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Authentication failed for https://github.com/example/repo.git')
+  })
 })

  39: export function defaultProjectWorktreePath(developmentPath: string): string {
  40:   return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'worktrees', slugifySegment(basename(resolve(developmentPath))))

  43: export function defaultProjectWorktreeBranch(): string {
  44:   return DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  45: }
  46: 
  47: export function resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>): string {
  48:   const requested = project.memory_branch.trim()
  49:   return isValidWorktreeBranchName(requested)
  50:     ? requested
  51:     : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  52: }
  53: 
  54: export function ensureProjectWorktree(project: ProjectEntry): WorktreeStatus {
  55:   const developmentPath = resolve(project.development_path)
  56:   const memoryPath = resolve(project.memory_path)
  57:   if (samePath(developmentPath, memoryPath)) {
  58:     return {
  59:       branch: git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim(),
  60:       created: false,

  71:       allowFailure: true,
  72:     }).trim()
  73:     if (!existingRoot) {
  74:       throw new Error(`Configured memory_path is not a git worktree: ${memoryPath}`)
  75:     }
  76:     const existingCommonDir = gitCommonDir(memoryPath)
  77:     if (!samePath(existingCommonDir, repoCommonDir)) {
  78:       throw new Error(`Configured memory_path belongs to a different repository: ${memoryPath}`)
  79:     }
  80: 
  81:     const current = git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
  82:     if (current !== branch) {
  83:       if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
  84:         git(memoryPath, 'checkout', branch)
  85:       } else {
  86:         git(memoryPath, 'checkout', '-b', branch)
  87:       }
  88:     }
  89: 
  90:     return { branch, created: false }
  91:   }
  92: 
  93:   mkdirSync(dirname(memoryPath), { recursive: true })
  94:   if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
  95:     git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', memoryPath, branch)
  96:   } else {
  97:     git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', '-b', branch, memoryPath, 'HEAD')
  98:   }
  99: 
 100:   return { branch, created: true }
 101: }
 102: 
 103: export function ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchUpstreamStatus {
 104:   const target = describePushRemote(projectPath)
 105:   if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
 106:     return {
 107:       remote: null,
 108:       created: false,
 109:       pushUrl: null,
 110:       transport: null,
 111:       usedFallback: false,
 112:     }
 113:   }
 114:   if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
 115:     return {
 116:       remote: target.remote,
 117:       created: false,
 118:       pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
 119:       transport: target.transport,
 120:       usedFallback: false,
 121:     }
 122:   }
 123: 
 124:   const resolvedTarget = {
 125:     ...target,
 126:     remote: target.remote,
 127:     pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
 128:     transport: target.transport,
 129:   }
 130:   const pushed = pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, resolvedTarget, true)
 131:   return {
 132:     remote: pushed.remote,
 133:     created: true,
 134:     pushUrl: pushed.pushUrl,
 135:     transport: pushed.transport,
 136:     usedFallback: pushed.usedFallback,
 137:   }
 138: }
 139: 
 140: export function pushBranchToRemote(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchPushStatus {
 141:   const remote = resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath) ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
 142:   const target = describePushRemote(projectPath, remote ?? undefined)
 143:   if (target.remote === null || target.pushUrl === null || target.transport === null) {
 144:     return {
 145:       remote: null,
 146:       pushUrl: null,
 147:       transport: null,
 148:       usedFallback: false,
 149:     }
 150:   }
 151: 
 152:   const resolvedTarget = {
 153:     ...target,
 154:     remote: target.remote,
 155:     pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
 156:     transport: target.transport,
 157:   }
 158:   return pushToResolvedRemote(projectPath, branch, resolvedTarget, false)
 159: }
 160: 
 161: export function describePushRemote(projectPath: string, remoteName?: string): PushRemoteStatus {
 162:   const remote = remoteName ?? resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
 163:   if (remote === null) {
 164:     return {
 165:       remote: null,
 166:       fetchUrl: null,
 167:       pushUrl: null,
 168:       transport: null,
 169:       fallbackUrls: [],
 170:     }
 171:   }
 172: 
 173:   const fetchUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', remote], {
 174:     cwd: projectPath,
 175:     allowFailure: true,
 176:   }).trim()
 177:   const pushUrl = execCommand('git', ['remote', 'get-url', '--push', remote], {
 178:     cwd: projectPath,
 179:     allowFailure: true,
 180:   }).trim() || fetchUrl
 181:   const transport = pushUrl ? detectPushTransport(pushUrl) : null
 182: 
 183:   return {
 184:     remote,
 185:     fetchUrl: fetchUrl || null,
 186:     pushUrl: pushUrl || null,
 187:     transport,
 188:     fallbackUrls: collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl, fetchUrl),
 189:   }
 190: }
 191: 
 192: export function resolvePushRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
 193:   const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
 194:     .split(/\r?\n/)
 195:     .map(remote => remote.trim())
 196:     .filter(Boolean)
 197: 
 198:   if (remotes.length === 0) {
 199:     return null
 200:   }
 201:   if (remotes.includes('origin')) {
 202:     return 'origin'
 203:   }
 204:   return remotes[0] ?? null
 205: }
 206: 
 207: export function hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath: string): boolean {
 208:   const output = execCommand(
 209:     'git',
 210:     ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
 211:     { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
 212:   )
 213:   return output.trim().length > 0
 214: }
 215: 
 216: export function isValidWorktreeBranchName(branch: string): boolean {
 217:   if (!branch || branch.trim() !== branch) {
 218:     return false
 219:   }
 220: 
 221:   const output = execCommand('git', ['check-ref-format', '--branch', branch], { allowFailure: true })
 222:   return output.trim() === branch
 223: }
 224: 
 225: export function isProjectMemoryBranch(
 226:   branch: string,
 227:   project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
 228: ): boolean {
 229:   const normalized = branch.trim()
 230:   if (!normalized) return false
 231: 
 232:   const expected = project ? resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project) : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
 233:   return normalized === expected
 234:     || normalized === DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
 235:     || normalized.startsWith(`${DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH}/`)
 236: }
 237: 
 238: function gitCommonDir(cwd: string): string {
 239:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--git-common-dir'], {
 240:     cwd,
 241:   }).trim()
 242: 
 243:   if (!output) {
 244:     throw new Error(`Unable to resolve git common-dir for: ${cwd}`)
 245:   }
 246: 
 247:   return isAbsolute(output) ? resolve(output) : resolve(cwd, output)
 248: }
 249: 
 250: function resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
 251:   const upstream = execCommand(
 252:     'git',
 253:     ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
 254:     { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
 255:   ).trim()
 256:   if (!upstream) {
 257:     return null
 258:   }
 259: 
 260:   const slashIndex = upstream.indexOf('/')
 261:   return slashIndex <= 0 ? null : upstream.slice(0, slashIndex)
 262: }
 263: 
 264: function pushToResolvedRemote(
 265:   projectPath: string,
 266:   branch: string,
 267:   target: PushRemoteStatus & { remote: string; pushUrl: string; transport: PushTransport },
 268:   setUpstream: boolean,
 269: ): BranchPushStatus {
 270:   const pushArgs = setUpstream
 271:     ? ['push', '-u', target.remote, branch]
 272:     : ['push', target.remote, branch]
 273: 
 274:   try {
 275:     git(projectPath, ...pushArgs)
 276:     return {
 277:       remote: target.remote,
 278:       pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
 279:       transport: target.transport,
 280:       usedFallback: false,
 281:     }
 282:   } catch (primaryError: unknown) {
 283:     if (shouldTryFallbackTransport(primaryError, target.fallbackUrls)) {
 284:       let lastFallbackFailure: { url: string; error: unknown } | null = null
 285:       for (const fallbackUrl of target.fallbackUrls) {
 286:         try {
 287:           execCommand(
 288:             'git',
 289:             ['-c', `remote.${target.remote}.pushurl=${fallbackUrl}`, ...pushArgs],
 290:             { cwd: projectPath },
 291:           )
 292:           return {
 293:             remote: target.remote,
 294:             pushUrl: fallbackUrl,
 295:             transport: detectPushTransport(fallbackUrl),
 296:             usedFallback: true,
 297:           }
 298:         } catch (fallbackError: unknown) {
 299:           lastFallbackFailure = {
 300:             url: fallbackUrl,
 301:             error: fallbackError,
 302:           }
 303:         }
 304:       }
 305:       if (lastFallbackFailure !== null) {
 306:         throw new Error(
 307:           formatPushFailure(
 308:             target.remote,
 309:             target.pushUrl,
 310:             primaryError,
 311:             lastFallbackFailure.url,
 312:             lastFallbackFailure.error,
 313:           ),
 314:         )
 315:       }
 316:     }
 317: 
 318:     throw new Error(formatPushFailure(target.remote, target.pushUrl, primaryError))
 319:   }
 320: }
 321: 
 322: function detectPushTransport(url: string): PushTransport {
 323:   const normalized = url.trim().toLowerCase()
 324:   if (normalized.startsWith('https://')) {
 325:     return 'https'
 326:   }
 327:   if (normalized.startsWith('ssh://') || normalized.startsWith('git@')) {
 328:     return 'ssh'
 329:   }
 330:   return 'other'
 331: }
 332: 
 333: function collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl: string, fetchUrl: string): string[] {
 334:   const candidates = [
 335:     fetchUrl,
 336:     ...alternateTransportUrls(pushUrl),
 337:     ...alternateTransportUrls(fetchUrl),
 338:   ]
 339: 
 340:   const seen = new Set<string>()
 341:   const normalizedPrimary = normalizeComparableUrl(pushUrl)
 342:   const fallbackUrls: string[] = []
 343: 
 344:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
 345:     const trimmed = candidate.trim()
 346:     if (!trimmed) continue
 347: 
 348:     const normalizedCandidate = normalizeComparableUrl(trimmed)
 349:     if (normalizedCandidate === normalizedPrimary || seen.has(normalizedCandidate)) {
 350:       continue
 351:     }
 352: 
 353:     seen.add(normalizedCandidate)
 354:     fallbackUrls.push(trimmed)
 355:   }
 356: 
 357:   return fallbackUrls
 358: }
 359: 
 360: function alternateTransportUrls(url: string): string[] {
 361:   const repo = parseGitHubRepo(url)
 362:   if (repo === null) {
 363:     return []
 364:   }
 365: 
 366:   const httpsUrl = `https://github.com/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
 367:   const sshUrl = `ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
 368: 
 369:   return [httpsUrl, sshUrl]
 370: }
 371: 
 372: function parseGitHubRepo(url: string): { owner: string; repo: string } | null {
 373:   const trimmed = url.trim()
 374:   if (!trimmed) {
 375:     return null
 376:   }
 377: 
 378:   const httpsMatch = trimmed.match(/^https:\/\/github\.com\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
 379:   if (httpsMatch) {
 380:     return {
 381:       owner: httpsMatch[1]!,
 382:       repo: httpsMatch[2]!,
 383:     }
 384:   }
 385: 
 386:   const scpMatch = trimmed.match(/^git@github\.com:([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?$/i)
 387:   if (scpMatch) {
 388:     return {
 389:       owner: scpMatch[1]!,
 390:       repo: scpMatch[2]!,
 391:     }
 392:   }
 393: 
 394:   const sshMatch = trimmed.match(/^ssh:\/\/git@(?:github\.com|ssh\.github\.com(?::443)?)\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
 395:   if (sshMatch) {
 396:     return {
 397:       owner: sshMatch[1]!,
 398:       repo: sshMatch[2]!,
 399:     }
 400:   }
 401: 
 402:   return null
 403: }
 404: 
 405: function shouldTryFallbackTransport(error: unknown, fallbackUrls: readonly string[]): boolean {
 406:   if (fallbackUrls.length === 0) {
 407:     return false
 408:   }
 409: 
 410:   const message = collectErrorText(error).toLowerCase()
 411:   return [
 412:     'host key verification failed',
 413:     'permission denied (publickey)',
 414:     'could not read from remote repository',
 415:     'authentication failed',
 416:     'repository not found',
 417:     'could not resolve host',
 418:     'unable to access',
 419:     'connection timed out',
 420:     'connection refused',
 421:     'ssh:',
 422:   ].some(fragment => message.includes(fragment))
 423: }
 424: 
 425: function formatPushFailure(
 426:   remote: string,
 427:   pushUrl: string,
 428:   primaryError: unknown,
 429:   fallbackUrl?: string,
 430:   fallbackError?: unknown,
 431: ): string {
 432:   const lines = [
 433:     `Unable to push branch to remote '${remote}'.`,
 434:     `Primary push URL: ${pushUrl}`,
 435:     `Primary error: ${summarizeError(primaryError)}`,
 436:   ]
 437: 
 438:   if (fallbackUrl) {
 439:     lines.push(`Fallback push URL: ${fallbackUrl}`)
 440:     if (fallbackError) {
 441:       lines.push(`Fallback error: ${summarizeError(fallbackError)}`)
 442:     }
 443:   }
 444: 
 445:   return lines.join('\n')
 446: }
 447: 
 448: function summarizeError(error: unknown): string {
 449:   const lines = collectErrorText(error)
 450:     .split(/\r?\n/)
 451:     .map(line => line.trim())
 452:     .filter(Boolean)
 453: 
 454:   const preferred = lines.find(line => !line.startsWith('Command failed:'))
 455:   return preferred ?? lines[0] ?? String(error)
 456: }
 457: 
 458: function collectErrorText(error: unknown): string {
 459:   if (error instanceof Error) {
 460:     const parts = [error.message]
 461:     const extra = error as {
 462:       readonly stdout?: string | Buffer
 463:       readonly stderr?: string | Buffer
 464:     }
 465:     if (extra.stdout !== undefined) {
 466:       parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stdout))
 467:     }
 468:     if (extra.stderr !== undefined) {
 469:       parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stderr))
 470:     }
 471:     return parts.filter(Boolean).join('\n')
 472:   }
 473: 
 474:   return String(error)
 475: }
 476: 
 477: function bufferToString(value: string | Buffer): string {
 478:   return typeof value === 'string' ? value : value.toString('utf-8')
 479: }
 480: 
 481: function normalizeComparableUrl(value: string): string {
 482:   return value.trim().replace(/\/+$/, '').toLowerCase()
 483: }
 484: 
 485: function branchExists(repoRoot: string, branch: string): boolean {
 486:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--verify', `refs/heads/${branch}`], {
 487:     cwd: repoRoot,
 488:     allowFailure: true,
 489:   })
 490:   return output.trim().length > 0
 491: }
 492: 
 493: function samePath(left: string, right: string): boolean {
 494:   return normalizePath(left) === normalizePath(right)
 495: }
 496: 
 497: function normalizePath(value: string): string {
 498:   let normalizedPath = resolve(value)
 499:   if (existsSync(normalizedPath)) {
 500:     try {
 501:       normalizedPath = realpathSync.native
 502:         ? realpathSync.native(normalizedPath)
 503:         : realpathSync(normalizedPath)
 504:     } catch {
 505:       // Fall back to the resolved path when realpath fails.
 506:     }
 507:   }
 508: 
 509:   const normalized = normalizedPath.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '')
 510:   return platform === 'win32' ? normalized.toLowerCase() : normalized
 511: }
 512: 
 513: function slugifySegment(value: string): string {
 514:   const slug = value
 515:     .trim()
 516:     .toLowerCase()
 517:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9._-]+/g, '-')
 518:     .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
 519:     .replace(/-{2,}/g, '-')
 520: 
 521:   return slug || 'project'
 522: }

1180: describe('cmdUninstall', () => {
1181:   let stderrChunks: string[]
1182:   let stdoutChunks: string[]
1183:   const originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write
1184:   const originalStdoutWrite = process.stdout.write
1185: 
1186:   afterEach(() => {
1187:     process.stderr.write = originalStderrWrite
1188:     process.stdout.write = originalStdoutWrite
1189:     vi.restoreAllMocks()
1190:   })
1191: 
1192:   it('returns 0 or 1 depending on platform', () => {
1193:     stderrChunks = []
1194:     stdoutChunks = []

1196:       stderrChunks.push(chunk)
1197:       return true
1198:     }) as typeof process.stderr.write

1200:       stdoutChunks.push(chunk)
1201:       return true
1202:     }) as typeof process.stdout.write
1203: 
1204:     const result = cmdUninstall()
1205:     // On any supported platform it returns 0; unsupported returns 1
1206:     expect([0, 1]).toContain(result)
1207:   })
1208: })

1213: 
1214: describe('writeVbsLauncher', () => {
1215:   it('writes a VBS file with UTF-16 LE BOM', () => {
1216:     const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher('C:\\test\\cli.js')
1217:     expect(existsSync(vbsPath)).toBe(true)
1218: 
1219:     const raw = readFileSync(vbsPath)
1220:     // UTF-16 LE BOM: 0xFF 0xFE
1221:     expect(raw[0]).toBe(0xff)
1222:     expect(raw[1]).toBe(0xfe)
1223: 
1224:     // Clean up
1225:     rmSync(vbsPath, { force: true })
1226:   })
1227: 
1228:   it('uses process.execPath instead of hardcoded node', () => {
1229:     const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher('C:\\test\\cli.js')
1230:     const raw = readFileSync(vbsPath)
1231:     // Decode UTF-16 LE (skip BOM)
1232:     const content = raw.subarray(2).toString('utf16le')
1233: 
1234:     // Should contain the full node path, not bare "node"
1235:     const expectedNode = process.execPath.replace(/"/g, '""')
1236:     expect(content).toContain(expectedNode)
1237:     expect(content).not.toMatch(/^WshShell\.Run "node /m)
1238: 
1239:     rmSync(vbsPath, { force: true })
1240:   })
1241: 
1242:   it('escapes double quotes in script path', () => {
1243:     const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher('C:\\path with "quotes"\\cli.js')
1244:     const raw = readFileSync(vbsPath)
1245:     const content = raw.subarray(2).toString('utf16le')
1246: 
1247:     // VBScript "" escaping
1248:     expect(content).toContain('path with ""quotes""')
1249: 
1250:     rmSync(vbsPath, { force: true })
1251:   })
1252: 
1253:   it('contains WScript.Shell and Run with SW_HIDE (0)', () => {
1254:     const vbsPath = writeVbsLauncher('C:\\test\\cli.js')
1255:     const raw = readFileSync(vbsPath)
1256:     const content = raw.subarray(2).toString('utf16le')
1257: 
1258:     expect(content).toContain('CreateObject("WScript.Shell")')
1259:     expect(content).toContain(', 0, False')
1260:     expect(content).toContain('daemon run-once')
1261: 
1262:     rmSync(vbsPath, { force: true })
1263:   })

1265:   it('vbsLauncherPath returns path under .memorytree', () => {
1266:     const p = vbsLauncherPath()
1267:     expect(p).toContain('.memorytree')
1268:     expect(p).toContain('heartbeat-launcher.vbs')
1269:   })
1270: })

  92: import { gitCommitAndPush, isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch, processProject } from '../../src/heartbeat/heartbeat.js'
  93: 
  94: function makeTranscript(): ParsedTranscript {
  95:   return {
  96:     client: 'codex',
  97:     session_id: 'sess-1',
  98:     title: 'Test session',
  99:     started_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
 100:     cwd: 'D:/repo',
 101:     branch: 'main',
 102:     messages: [{ role: 'user', text: 'hello', timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' }],
 103:     tool_events: [],
 104:     source_path: 'C:/tmp/rollout-1.jsonl',
 105:   }
 106: }
 107: 
 108: beforeEach(() => {
 109:   vi.clearAllMocks()
 110: 
 111:   mocks.discoverSourceFiles.mockReturnValue([['codex', 'C:/tmp/rollout-1.jsonl']])
 112:   mocks.transcriptMatchesRepo.mockReturnValue(true)
 113:   mocks.defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot.mockReturnValue('C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/transcripts')
 114:   mocks.transcriptHasContent.mockReturnValue(true)
 115:   mocks.parseTranscript.mockReturnValue(makeTranscript())
 116:   mocks.importTranscript.mockResolvedValue({})
 117:   mocks.buildReport.mockResolvedValue(undefined)
 118:   mocks.slugify.mockReturnValue('demo-project')
 119:   mocks.loadConfig.mockReturnValue({})
 120:   mocks.acquireLock.mockReturnValue(true)
 121:   mocks.toPosixPath.mockImplementation((value: string) => value)
 122:   mocks.ensureBranchUpstream.mockReturnValue({ remote: 'origin', created: true })
 123:   mocks.pushBranchToRemote.mockReturnValue({
 124:     remote: 'origin',
 125:     pushUrl: 'https://github.com/example/repo.git',
 126:     transport: 'https',
 127:     usedFallback: false,
 128:   })
 129:   mocks.hasTrackingUpstream.mockReturnValue(true)
 130:   mocks.isProjectMemoryBranch.mockImplementation((branch: string) => (
 131:     branch === 'memorytree' || branch.startsWith('memorytree/')
 132:   ))
 133: 
 134:   mocks.getLogger.mockReturnValue({
 135:     info: vi.fn(),
 136:     warn: vi.fn(),
 137:     debug: vi.fn(),
 138:     error: vi.fn(),
 139:     exception: vi.fn(),
 140:   })
 141: })
 142: 
 143: describe('isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch', () => {
 144:   it('matches memorytree and legacy memorytree/* branches', () => {
 145:     expect(isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch('memorytree')).toBe(true)
 146:     expect(isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch('memorytree/transcripts')).toBe(true)
 147:     expect(isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch('memorytree/openmnemo')).toBe(true)
 148:     expect(isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch('main')).toBe(false)
 149:     expect(isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch('feature/memorytree')).toBe(false)
 150:   })
 151: })
 152: 
 153: describe('processProject branch safety', () => {
 154:   it('imports to global archive only when the current branch is not memorytree/*', async () => {
 155:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 156:       if (args[0] === 'rev-parse') return 'main\n'
 157:       throw new Error(`unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 158:     })
 159: 
 160:     await processProject({ auto_push: true } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project')
 161: 
 162:     expect(mocks.importTranscript).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
 163:     expect(mocks.importTranscript.mock.calls[0]?.[5]).toBe(false)
 164:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
 165:   })
 166: 
 167:   it('allows repo-local commit and push when already on a memorytree/* branch', async () => {
 168:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 169:       if (args[0] === 'rev-parse') return 'memorytree/transcripts\n'
 170:       if (args[0] === 'diff') {
 171:         return [
 172:           'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2024/01/file.md',
 173:         ].join('\n')
 174:       }
 175:       if (args[0] === 'ls-files' && args.includes('--ignored')) {
 176:         return 'Memory/06_transcripts/raw/codex/2024/01/file.jsonl\n'
 177:       }
 178:       if (args[0] === 'ls-files') return 'Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2024/01/file.json\n'
 179:       if (args[0] === 'remote') return 'origin\n'
 180:       return ''
 181:     })
 182: 
 183:     await processProject({ auto_push: true } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project')
 184: 
 185:     expect(mocks.importTranscript).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
 186:     expect(mocks.importTranscript.mock.calls[0]?.[5]).toBe(true)
 187:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
 188:       'D:/repo',
 189:       'add',
 190:       '-A',
 191:       '-f',
 192:       '--',
 193:       'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2024/01/file.md',
 194:       'Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2024/01/file.json',
 195:     )
 196:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'commit', '-m', 'memorytree(transcripts): import 1 transcript(s)')
 197:     expect(mocks.pushBranchToRemote).toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'memorytree/transcripts')
 198:   })
 199: 
 200:   it('builds the report before committing imported transcripts on a memorytree branch', async () => {
 201:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 202:       if (args[0] === 'rev-parse') return 'memorytree/transcripts\n'
 203:       if (args[0] === 'diff') {
 204:         return [
 205:           'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2024/01/file.md',
 206:         ].join('\n')
 207:       }
 208:       if (args[0] === 'ls-files') return 'Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/codex/2024/01/file.json\n'
 209:       return ''
 210:     })
 211: 
 212:     await processProject({ auto_push: false, generate_report: true } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project')
 213: 
 214:     expect(mocks.buildReport).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
 215:     const commitCallIndex = mocks.git.mock.calls.findIndex(([, ...args]) => args[0] === 'commit')
 216:     expect(commitCallIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
 217:     const buildOrder = mocks.buildReport.mock.invocationCallOrder[0]!
 218:     const commitOrder = mocks.git.mock.invocationCallOrder[commitCallIndex]!
 219:     expect(buildOrder).toBeLessThan(commitOrder)
 220:   })
 221: 
 222:   it('commits a snapshot when no new transcripts were imported but context changed', async () => {
 223:     mocks.discoverSourceFiles.mockReturnValue([])
 224:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 225:       if (args[0] === 'rev-parse') return 'memorytree/transcripts\n'
 226:       if (args[0] === 'diff') return 'Memory/02_todos/todo_v001_001_20260317.md\n'
 227:       return ''
 228:     })
 229: 
 230:     await processProject({ auto_push: false, generate_report: false } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project')
 231: 
 232:     expect(mocks.importTranscript).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
 233:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
 234:       'D:/repo',
 235:       'add',
 236:       '-A',
 237:       '-f',
 238:       '--',
 239:       'Memory/02_todos/todo_v001_001_20260317.md',
 240:     )
 241:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'commit', '-m', 'memorytree(snapshot): heartbeat sync')
 242:   })
 243: 
 244:   it('commits AGENTS.md changes on a memorytree branch', async () => {
 245:     mocks.discoverSourceFiles.mockReturnValue([])
 246:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 247:       if (args[0] === 'rev-parse') return 'memorytree/transcripts\n'
 248:       if (args[0] === 'diff') return 'AGENTS.md\n'
 249:       return ''
 250:     })
 251: 
 252:     await processProject({ auto_push: false, generate_report: false } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project')
 253: 
 254:     expect(mocks.importTranscript).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
 255:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
 256:       'D:/repo',
 257:       'add',
 258:       '-A',
 259:       '-f',
 260:       '--',
 261:       'AGENTS.md',
 262:     )
 263:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'commit', '-m', 'memorytree(snapshot): heartbeat sync')
 264:   })
 265: })
 266: 
 267: describe('gitCommitAndPush raw transcript staging', () => {
 268:   it('skips commit when only raw transcript mirror files changed', () => {
 269:     mocks.git.mockImplementation((_cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 270:       if (args[0] === 'ls-files' && args.includes('--ignored')) {
 271:         return 'Memory/06_transcripts/raw/codex/2024/01/file.jsonl\n'
 272:       }
 273:       if (args[0] === 'remote') return 'origin\n'
 274:       return ''
 275:     })
 276: 
 277:     gitCommitAndPush({ auto_push: true } as never, 'D:/repo', 'demo-project', 1)
 278: 
 279:     expect(mocks.git).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
 280:       'D:/repo',
 281:       'ls-files',
 282:       '--others',
 283:       '--ignored',
 284:       '--exclude-standard',
 285:       '--',
 286:       'AGENTS.md',
 287:       'Memory/01_goals',
 288:       'Memory/02_todos',
 289:       'Memory/03_chat_logs',
 290:       'Memory/04_knowledge',
 291:       'Memory/05_archive',
 292:       'Memory/06_transcripts',
 293:       'Memory/07_reports',
 294:     )
 295:     expect(mocks.git).not.toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'commit', '-m', 'memorytree(transcripts): import 1 transcript(s)')
 296:     expect(mocks.git).not.toHaveBeenCalledWith('D:/repo', 'push')
 297:   })
 298: })

 343: export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
 344:   try {
 345:     const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)
 346:     if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
 347:       const pushed = pushBranchToRemote(projectPath, branch)
 348:       if (pushed.remote === null) {
 349:         return false
 350:       }
 351:       if (pushed.usedFallback && pushed.pushUrl !== null) {
 352:         getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully via fallback ${pushed.pushUrl}.`)
 353:       }
 354:     } else {
 355:       const upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath, branch)
 356:       if (upstream.remote === null) {
 357:         return false
 358:       }
 359:       const fallbackNote = upstream.usedFallback && upstream.pushUrl !== null
 360:         ? ` via fallback ${upstream.pushUrl}`
 361:         : ''
 362:       getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Configured upstream ${upstream.remote}/${branch}${fallbackNote}.`)
 363:     }
 364:     getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
 365:     return true
 366:   } catch {
